tools.name: burp_help
tools.title: Help & Discovery
tools.description: Discover and learn about all Burp MCP capabilities. Use this tool to list available tools, search by capability, or get detailed help for a specific tool. Returns tool names, descriptions, and examples. Always start here when unsure which tool to use.

tool.description: Specific tool name to get detailed help for (e.g., 'burp_scanner')

capability.description: Search by what you want to do (e.g., 'scan for vulnerabilities', 'send HTTP requests')

list.description: Set to true to list all available tools with summaries

section.description: Which section to return: full (all info), examples (usage examples), parameters (input schema), summary (brief overview)

tools.name: burp_proxy_history
tools.title: Proxy History
tools.description: Retrieve and filter HTTP proxy history captured by Burp Suite. Use this to analyze traffic patterns, find specific requests, or extract data from past sessions. Supports 25+ filters including hostname, method, status code, path, parameters, and regex patterns. Returns request/response pairs with timing data. Use 'list' mode for URLs only, 'detail' for full content, or 'iterate' for sequential browsing.

action.description: Mode: list (URLs only), detail (full request/response for specific IDs), iterate (sequential browsing)

count.description: Number of entries to return in iterate mode

limit.description: Maximum entries for list mode (no upper limit)

method.description: Filter by HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.)

parameterType.description: Parameter location (URL, BODY, COOKIE, JSON, XML, MULTIPART)

inScopeOnly.description: Only return items in target scope

contentType.description: Filter by content type (JSON, XML, URL_ENCODED, MULTIPART, AMF)

afterTime.description: Filter requests after this time (ISO-8601 or relative like '1h', '30m', '7d')

beforeTime.description: Filter requests before this time (ISO-8601 format)

modifiedOnly.description: Show only requests that were modified

tools.name: burp_repeater
tools.title: Repeater (UI Only)
tools.description: Opens Burp Repeater UI tab for manual testing. WARNING: This tool CANNOT send HTTP requests programmatically - it only creates UI tabs for human interaction. To actually send and receive HTTP responses, use burp_custom_http instead. Use this only when preparing requests for manual testing in the Burp UI.

action.description: SEND_TO_REPEATER creates a new tab, SEND_FROM_PROXY sends existing proxy item

proxyUrl.description: URL from proxy history to send (for SEND_FROM_PROXY)

tools.name: burp_proxy_interceptor
tools.title: Proxy Interceptor
tools.description: Real-time proxy request interception and modification with event-driven control. Use this to intercept requests as they pass through Burp Proxy, inspect them, and decide to forward, drop, or modify. Actions: enable/disable (MCP interception), master_intercept_on/off (Burp UI button), get_queue (pending requests), modify/forward/drop_request. Note: Response modification not yet implemented.

request_id.description: ID of the request to modify (from get_queue)

modifications.description: Modifications to apply (add_headers, remove_headers, replace_body, method, path)

tools.name: burp_global_interceptor
tools.title: Global Interceptor
tools.description: Global HTTP and WebSocket interceptor for ALL Burp tools (Scanner, Intruder, Repeater, etc.). Use this for global authentication injection, header modification across all tools, response manipulation, and WebSocket message interception. Supports rule-based automatic mode or event-driven manual control. Common use cases: add auth to Scanner, inject headers in WebSocket handshakes, WAF bypass testing.
List of tools to filter (PROXY, SCANNER, INTRUDER, etc.)

tools.name: burp_custom_http
tools.title: HTTP Client (Primary)
tools.description: PRIMARY TOOL for sending HTTP requests and receiving responses programmatically. DEFAULT: HTTPS on port 443. For HTTP, use port 80 in Host header or http:// URL scheme. Actions: SEND_REQUEST (single request), SEND_PARALLEL (race condition testing), TOGGLE_REQUEST_METHOD (GET<>POST), ANALYZE_PROTOCOL (detect HTTP/HTTPS). Supports HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, SNI, redirects, and connection reuse. Use this instead of burp_repeater for automated testing.

request.description: Raw HTTP request string with headers and body

requests.description: Array of requests for SEND_PARALLEL (race condition testing)

connection_id.description: Unique identifier for connection reuse. Requests with same ID use same TCP connection

upstream_tls_verification.description: Enable TLS certificate verification for upstream servers

allow_h2c.description: Allow HTTP/2 cleartext (h2c) mode for non-TLS connections

add_to_sitemap.description: Add request/response to Burp Site Map

use_cookie_jar.description: Include cookies from Burp's cookie jar. Request cookies take precedence

use_proxy_headers.description: Copy headers from recent proxy history for realistic browser traffic

response_timeout.description: Max time to wait for response in milliseconds

server_name_indicator.description: Custom SNI hostname for TLS connections

tools.name: burp_scanner
tools.title: Vulnerability Scanner
tools.description: Comprehensive vulnerability scanner and crawler with full automation. Use this to scan targets for security vulnerabilities, discover content via crawling, and retrieve scan results. Actions: START_SCAN (scan with optional crawl), CRAWL_ONLY (content discovery), GET_STATUS (check progress), GET_ISSUES (retrieve vulnerabilities), CANCEL_SCAN, LIST_SCANS, GENERATE_REPORT. Supports authenticated scanning via headers/cookies parameters. Use insertionPoints for targeted parameter testing.

mode.description: ACTIVE sends test payloads, PASSIVE only analyzes existing traffic

request.description: HTTP request to add to scan

insertionPoints.description: Insertion point ranges [{start: int, end: int}]

port.description: Target port (default: 80 for HTTP, 443 for HTTPS)

urlFilter.description: URL pattern filter with wildcards (for FILTER_ISSUES/GET_ISSUES)

useProxySession.description: Automatically use authenticated session from proxy history (default: true). Finds the most recent valid request to the target URL and extracts all headers/cookies. Set to false to use basic unauthenticated requests.

tools.name: burp_intruder
tools.title: Intruder (UI Only)
tools.description: Configures Burp Intruder UI for attack setup. WARNING: This tool CANNOT execute attacks programmatically - it only configures the UI. You must manually add payloads and click 'Start attack' in Burp UI. For automated parameter fuzzing/testing, use burp_custom_http with a loop instead. Use this only when setting up complex attacks for manual execution.

action.description: SEND_TO_INTRUDER creates tab, SEND_WITH_POSITIONS marks attack positions

body.description: Request body with positions marked using section sign characters

tools.name: burp_add_issue
tools.title: Create Issue
tools.description: Add custom audit issues to Burp Suite with intelligent dynamic grouping. Use this to report security findings discovered through manual testing or external tools. Automatically groups with existing issues of the same type. Supports detailed evidence including request/response pairs, payloads, remediation guidance, and references. Issues appear in Burp's Target > Issues panel.

url.description: URL where the issue was found

issueType.description: Vulnerability type (e.g., 'SQL injection', 'XSS'). Auto-groups with similar issues.

detail.description: Detailed description of this specific instance

request.description: Raw HTTP request that triggered the issue

response.description: Raw HTTP response showing the vulnerability

name.description: Specific issue instance description (optional - will be added to detail if provided)

background.description: Technical background about this vulnerability type (AI can provide context)

references.description: References and links for further reading (OWASP, CWE, etc.)

payload.description: The actual payload used in the attack (will be bolded in detail)

requests.description: Evidence requests. Each entry can be a raw request string or an object (e.g. {"raw": "..."}). Objects may also include metadata fields like 'id' or 'notes' – only 'raw'/'request' are required to build evidence.

responses.description: Evidence responses matching the requests array. Accepts raw strings or objects with a 'raw'/'response' field.

filters.description: ProxyHistoryTool filters for finding request in proxy history (method, parameter, contains, etc.) - see burp_proxy_history for all options. Use when you want to match specific proxy history entries.

tools.name: burp_session_management
tools.title: Session Manager
tools.description: Advanced session management with Burp's native cookie jar and automatic session handling. Use this to manage cookies, extract tokens, test session validity, and configure automatic session refresh. Actions: COOKIE_JAR_LIST/SET/DELETE (manage cookies), EXTRACT_TOKENS (find session tokens), TEST_SESSION (verify session validity), ENABLE/DISABLE_AUTO_SESSION (automatic refresh on 401/403). Integrates with Burp's session handling rules.

extractPattern.description: Regex pattern to extract tokens (for EXTRACT_TOKENS)

expiration.description: Cookie expiration in ISO-8601 format (for COOKIE_JAR_SET, optional)

autoRefresh.description: Enable automatic token refresh on 401/403 responses

validityKeywords.description: Keywords to check for session validity

tools.name: burp_comparer
tools.title: Response Comparer
tools.description: Compare HTTP requests, responses, and other data. Use this to identify differences between two pieces of data, useful for finding subtle changes in responses that indicate vulnerabilities. Actions: COMPARE_RESPONSES, COMPARE_REQUESTS, COMPARE_TEXT, SEND_TO_COMPARER (opens Burp UI), COMPARE_PROXY_ENTRIES. Supports word-level and byte-level comparison with optional whitespace ignoring.

url1.description: First URL for comparison (for proxy entry comparison)

url2.description: Second URL for comparison (for proxy entry comparison)

tools.name: burp_collaborator
tools.title: Collaborator (OOB)
tools.description: Generate payloads and monitor out-of-band interactions for blind vulnerability testing. Use this for SSRF, blind XXE, blind SQL injection, and other vulnerabilities that require external callback verification. Actions: GENERATE_PAYLOAD (create unique callback URLs), CHECK_INTERACTIONS (poll for callbacks), LIST_PAYLOADS, SERVER_INFO. Professional license required. Supports DNS, HTTP, HTTPS, and SMTP interactions.

payloadId.description: Specific payload ID to check interactions for

customData.description: Custom data to include with payload for tracking purposes

secretKey.description: Secret key for restoring a collaborator client session

tools.name: burp_scope
tools.title: Target Scope
tools.description: Manage Burp Suite target scope to control which URLs are included in testing. Use this to add/remove URLs from scope, check if URLs are in scope, and analyze scope coverage against proxy history. Actions: view (current scope with stats), add (include URL), remove (exclude URL), check (verify URL status), analyze (scope coverage report), bulk_add/bulk_check.

urls.description: Array of URLs for bulk operations

tools.name: burp_organizer
tools.title: Request Organizer
tools.description: Manage and organize HTTP requests/responses in Burp's Organizer tool. Use this to bookmark interesting requests, track testing progress, and organize findings. Actions: SEND_TO_ORGANIZER (add requests), LIST_ITEMS (view all with filters), GET_ITEM_BY_ID, GET_ITEM_COUNT, GET_ITEM_STATUS. Supports status types: NEW, IN_PROGRESS, POSTPONED, DONE, IGNORED.

url.description: URL to send to organizer or filter by

fromProxy.description: Send from proxy history instead of creating new request

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return

urlPattern.description: URL pattern to filter (supports partial matching)

methodFilter.description: HTTP method to filter by (for LIST_ITEMS_FILTERED)

itemId.description: Item ID for GET_ITEM_BY_ID or GET_ITEM_STATUS

tools.name: burp_annotate
tools.title: Annotations
tools.description: Add notes and highlight colors to entries across all Burp Suite components. Use this to mark interesting items, add testing notes, and organize findings visually. Supports Proxy, Target, Organizer, Repeater, Intruder, Scanner, WebSocket, and Collaborator. Actions: ANNOTATE_* (per component), GET_ANNOTATIONS, ANNOTATE_BY_PATTERN (bulk), SEARCH_BY_ANNOTATION, ENABLE/DISABLE_AUTO_ANNOTATION.

notes.description: Notes to add to the item

pattern.description: Pattern to match URLs for bulk annotation

tools.name: burp_sitemap_analysis
tools.title: Site Map Analysis
tools.description: Analyze site structure, detect technologies, map attack surface, and identify potential vulnerabilities from captured traffic. Actions: ANALYZE_STRUCTURE (directory tree), DETECT_TECHNOLOGY (stack fingerprinting), MAP_ATTACK_SURFACE (input points), CONTENT_ANALYSIS (sensitive data), RESPONSE_STATS (metrics), CORRELATE_ISSUES, CREATE_ISSUES_FROM_ANALYSIS (auto-create issues for findings).

target.description: Target URL prefix to analyze (e.g., https://example.com)

analyzeKeywords.description: Keywords to analyze for variance (for KEYWORD_ANALYSIS action)

tools.name: burp_bambda
tools.title: Bambda Filters
tools.description: Create and apply advanced Bambda filters for intelligent traffic filtering using Java code. Bambdas are powerful filters written in Java that can filter Proxy history, Site map, and Logger. Actions: APPLY_FILTER (use preset or custom), LIST_PRESETS (available filters), CREATE_CUSTOM (write Java filter), GET_ACTIVE_FILTER. Presets include: authenticated_requests, api_endpoints, sql_injection_candidates, error_responses, xss_candidates.

tools.name: burp_logs
tools.title: Extension Logs
tools.description: Access and manage Burp Suite extension logs. Use this to retrieve output logs, error logs, write custom log entries, and raise debug events. Actions: GET_LOGS (retrieve logs), WRITE_LOG (add entry), RAISE_EVENT (debug/info/error/critical), CLEAR_LOGS. Useful for debugging and monitoring extension behavior.

level.description: Log level for WRITE_LOG or RAISE_EVENT

object.description: Complex object to log (JSON, array, etc.) - automatically converted to string

exceptionMessage.description: Exception message for ERROR level logging

tools.name: burp_websocket
tools.title: WebSocket Client
tools.description: View WebSocket proxy history and create WebSocket connections. Use this to analyze captured WebSocket traffic, create new WebSocket connections, send messages, and manage connection lifecycle. Actions: proxy_history (view captured messages), create (new connection), send (send message), close (disconnect), list_connections.

upgradeRequest.description: Optional HTTP upgrade request for create action

tools.name: burp_websocket_interceptor
tools.title: WebSocket Interceptor
tools.description: Intercept and modify WebSocket messages in real-time. Use this for testing WebSocket-based applications by intercepting, modifying, and filtering messages. Supports text and binary messages with match/replace rules. Actions: enable/disable, status, get_queue, forward/drop/modify (message control), add/remove_filter, add/remove_auto_modify (automatic replacements).

new_payload.description: New payload for modify (text or Base64 for binary)

filter_pattern.description: Regex pattern to match messages. For binary messages, pattern is matched against hex representation

rule_name.description: Name for the auto-modify rule (text messages only)

search_pattern.description: Pattern to search for in text messages

tools.name: burp_response_analyzer
tools.title: Response Analyzer
tools.description: Analyze HTTP responses for keywords, variations, reflection points, and anomalies. Use this to find dynamic content, identify reflection points for XSS testing, detect security-related keywords, and rank anomalous responses. Actions: keywords (find security terms), variations (detect dynamic content), reflection (XSS testing), pattern (regex search), rank_anomalies (AI-powered anomaly detection), all (complete analysis).

topN.description: Number of top anomalies to return

urls.description: URLs to analyze for response variations (for variations action)

caseSensitive.description: Whether pattern matching should be case sensitive

tools.name: burp_utilities
tools.title: Utilities
tools.description: Access Burp's utility functions for encoding/decoding, hashing, random data generation, JSON manipulation, and shell execution. Actions: base64_encode/decode, url_encode/decode, html_encode/decode, hash (MD5/SHA), random (generate data), json_beautify/validate/path, hex_to_ascii, number_convert, shell_execute (run commands). Essential helper functions for payload crafting and data manipulation.

commandArgs.description: Command arguments as separate strings (safer than single string)

timeout.description: Command timeout in seconds (default: 10, 0 to disable)

allowNonZeroExit.description: Allow non-zero exit codes without error

allowTimeout.description: Silently handle timeout instead of error

searchPattern.description: Pattern to search for in bytes (string or regex)

categories.name: Core HTTP/Proxy
categories.description: HTTP operations, proxy control, and traffic interception

categories.name: Response Analysis
categories.description: Response content analysis and anomaly detection
